behaviorsAudited.behavior: LLMStreamChunk in runtime/src/llm/types.ts gains optional toolInputBlockStart and toolInputDelta fields whose payloads carry {callId, index, contentBlock} and {callId, index, partialJson} respectively, mirroring the upstream content_block_start (tool_use) and input_json_delta data shapes so the AgenC TUI bridge can reconstruct the upstream streamingToolUses contract from messages.ts:3024-3079
behaviorsAudited.assertedAt: runtime/src/llm/providers/anthropic/adapter.input-json-delta-forwarding.parity.test.ts:E6.4 + adapter.test.ts:streams messages-api text deltas and emits final tool calls from tool_use blocks

behaviorsAudited.behavior: The Anthropic adapter (adapter.ts) emits a chunk with toolInputBlockStart whenever it receives an SSE content_block_start whose content_block.type === 'tool_use' and a non-negative index — fired BEFORE any input_json_delta for that block reaches the consumer
behaviorsAudited.assertedAt: runtime/src/llm/providers/anthropic/adapter.input-json-delta-forwarding.parity.test.ts:B6.2 toolInputBlockStart fires BEFORE the first toolInputDelta for the same block

behaviorsAudited.behavior: The Anthropic adapter emits a chunk with toolInputDelta for each content_block_delta whose delta.type === 'input_json_delta' and a non-negative index, carrying the raw partial_json string, in addition to (not in place of) its existing toolBlocks.arguments accumulation
behaviorsAudited.assertedAt: runtime/src/llm/providers/anthropic/adapter.input-json-delta-forwarding.parity.test.ts:E6.4 multiple input_json_delta events forward in arrival order

behaviorsAudited.behavior: Forwarded events do not break the existing completed-tool-call emission at content_block_stop (current behavior at adapter.ts ~lines 292-311 must still emit chunk.toolCalls)
behaviorsAudited.assertedAt: runtime/src/llm/providers/anthropic/adapter.input-json-delta-forwarding.parity.test.ts:E6.6 + runtime/src/llm/providers/anthropic/adapter.test.ts:streams messages-api text deltas and emits final tool calls

behaviorsAudited.behavior: Existing onChunk text-delta path is unaffected (no regression in streaming text rendering)
behaviorsAudited.assertedAt: runtime/src/llm/providers/anthropic/adapter.input-json-delta-forwarding.parity.test.ts:B6.5 the existing onChunk text-delta path is unaffected when tool_use blocks are interleaved with text

behaviorsAudited.behavior: stream-model.ts onChunk handler emits a session event {type:'tool_input_block_start', payload:{callId,index,contentBlock}} when chunk.toolInputBlockStart is set, and a session event {type:'tool_input_delta', payload:{callId,index,partialJson}} when chunk.toolInputDelta is set; both feed the message-adapter accumulator from R5

event-log.ts EventMsg discriminated union and KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES set declare both tool_input_block_start and tool_input_delta variants so the typed Session.emit accepts the new events without falling into the unknown-tag I-26 shim path
behaviorsAudited.assertedAt: runtime/src/session/event-log.tool-input-events.parity.test.ts: 5 tests covering isKnownEventType('tool_input_block_start')/('tool_input_delta'), KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES.has() membership for both, and EventMsg discriminated-union typed-assignment checks for the documented payload shapes ({callId,index,contentBlock} for block_start; {callId,index,partialJson} for delta)

edgeCasesAudited.edgeCase: An SSE content_block_start with content_block.type !== 'tool_use' does NOT emit toolInputBlockStart (only tool_use blocks open a streaming-tool-use entry; text blocks must not appear in transcript.streamingToolUses)
An SSE content_block_start with index === -1 (the adapter's sentinel for missing index) does NOT emit toolInputBlockStart (only valid indices reach the bridge)
An input_json_delta whose partial_json is not a string is dropped at the adapter (matches the existing typeof guard at adapter.ts:285); no chunk.toolInputDelta is emitted
Multiple input_json_deltas for the same tool block in rapid succession all forward through onChunk in arrival order with their partial_json contents preserved
Two concurrent tool_use blocks at distinct indices each emit their own toolInputBlockStart and their own toolInputDelta chunks without interleaving payloads
A consumer that ignores chunk.toolInputBlockStart and chunk.toolInputDelta still observes the existing completed-tool-call emission at content_block_stop (backward-compatibility)
stream-model.ts onChunk handler does NOT emit a session event when chunk.toolInputBlockStart and chunk.toolInputDelta are both undefined (only the existing assistant-text and tool-call paths fire)

The new chunk fields are opt-in for non-Anthropic providers — providers that do not set them produce no streaming-tool-use UI, but their existing tool-call emission is unaffected (matches the matrix scope statement that openai/grok/etc. are out of scope for this contract)
edgeCasesAudited.assertedAt: runtime/src/phases/stream-model.tool-input-events.parity.test.ts:E6.8 chunks emitted by non-Anthropic providers that do not set the new fields never produce streaming-tool-use session events

hiddenCoupling.note: The matrix references the typeof guard 'at adapter.ts:285' but in the current adapter.ts the typeof delta.partial_json === 'string' guard sits at line 308 (line 285 is part of the toolInputBlockStart emission). The guard is correctly preserved and E6.3 asserts it; the line number in the matrix doc is stale but the behavior is correct.

EventMsg payloads use 'readonly' fields whereas LLMStreamChunk.toolInputBlockStart and LLMStreamChunk.toolInputDelta use mutable fields with structurally identical shapes. TypeScript permits assignment from mutable to readonly so the session.emit({type:'tool_input_block_start', payload: chunk.toolInputBlockStart}) and ({type:'tool_input_delta', payload: chunk.toolInputDelta}) call sites at stream-model.ts:287/288/296/297 typecheck. Verified end-to-end by running `npx tsc --noEmit` at runtime/ which exits clean.

summary: Row anthropic-input-json-delta-forwarding is fully implemented and asserted across all four target files, addressing the contract reviewer's prior finding that tool_input_block_start and tool_input_delta were not declared in the typed EventMsg union. runtime/src/session/event-log.ts now declares both variants in the EventMsg discriminated union (lines 484-504) with payload shapes {callId,index,contentBlock:{type:'tool_use',id,name,input}} and {callId,index,partialJson}, and registers both tags in KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES (lines 709-710) so the I-26 unknown-tag shim is bypassed. The new dedicated test file runtime/src/session/event-log.tool-input-events.parity.test.ts adds 5 tests asserting isKnownEventType returns true for both tags, KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES.has() membership for both, and discriminated-union typed-assignment for both payload shapes; all 5 pass. The remaining files are unchanged from the prior APPROVED state: runtime/src/llm/types.ts:661-685 carries the chunk-side fields with structurally identical shapes (mutable → readonly assignment is valid), runtime/src/llm/providers/anthropic/adapter.ts:257-286 and 306-323 emit the chunks at the correct SSE boundaries, and runtime/src/phases/stream-model.ts:279-301 fans them into the typed session events at the call site (line 444). `npx tsc --noEmit` at runtime/ now passes (verified live, zero errors), confirming the stream-model session.emit calls at lines 287/288/296/297 typecheck against the now-extended EventMsg union. All three R6 parity test files run green: 18 tests total (8 adapter + 5 stream-model + 5 event-log). One low-severity note: the matrix's typeof guard line reference 'adapter.ts:285' is stale (now line 308); behavior is correct.
